title: anthyllis vulneraria subsp. argyrophylla
intro: Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. argyrophylla, also known as Kidney Vetch or Mountain Kidney Vetch, is a flowering plant species of the legume family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of kidney stones. The herbal preparations of Anthyllis vulneraria subsp. argyrophylla include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas or taken as supplements under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
